content.problemStatement:
Today is the Robolympics athletic event. **N** batons numbered 0 to **N - 1** are placed in a circular fashion where baton numbers increase in clockwise direction. Also, some of the batons are special. **M** robots start in the event by standing near a baton position and picking it up. Note that a baton cannot be held by more than one robot, therefore all robots have distinct positions in the circular array of batons.

Now, this event lasts for **N** seconds. In each second, each robot drops the baton currently in its hand and moves to next position in clockwise direction i.e. if a robot has baton **i** in its hand at **t = 0**, at **t = 1** he will be holding baton **(i + 1) % N**.


Crowd goes berserk whenever there comes a moment when all robots have picked up special batons. Your aim is to count how many times it will happen during the whole race. Note that crowd can go berserk even at **t = 0**. But since race is over at **t = N**, crowd doesn't care anymore. e. The crowd will not go berserk at **t = N**.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains two space-separated integers N and M, where N is the number of batons and M is the number of robots. The second line contains N space-separated integers where each integer is 0 or 1, denoting whether the baton is special or not. The third line contains M space-separated integers denoting the initial positions of the robots.
content.outputFormat: The output is a single integer denoting the number of times the crowd goes berserk during the whole race.

sampleTestCases.explanation: At t = 0 and t = 2, the robot is holding a special baton.
At t = 1 and t = 3, both robots are holding special batons.

solution.approach: The approach is to simulate the movement of the robots and check at each time step if all robots are holding special batons. We can use a circular array to represent the batons and the robots' positions. We iterate over each time step, update the robots' positions, and check if all robots are holding special batons. If they are, we increment the count of times the crowd goes berserk.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) — the time complexity is linear with respect to the number of batons because we simulate the movement of the robots for N time steps.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — the space complexity is linear with respect to the number of batons because we need to store the special batons and the robots' positions.

code.Python3:
import sys
N, M = map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split())
special = list(map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split()))
robots = list(map(int, sys.stdin.readline().split()))
count = 0
for t in range(N):
    allSpecial = 1
    for i in range(M):
        if special[(robots[i] + t) % N] == 0:
            allSpecial = 0
            break
    if allSpecial == 1:
        count += 1
print(count)
